summary: Large gm_18A5 decomp/matching PR for the game-mode tournament/bracket area. It replaced many placeholder functions in src/melee/gm/gm_18A5.c with C implementations, updated gm_18A5 headers/types, and made a small matching-related change in gmtou.c. The bot report recorded +7748 matched bytes, 18 new full matches, and 46 unmatched-item improvements, mostly in main/melee/gm/gm_18A5.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation target; large replacement of placeholder functions with C implementations, added includes, externs, static GXColor data, bracket layout/drawing logic, JObj translation calls, and PAD_STACK in fn_8018A514.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_18A5.c include additions gobjplink.h and lbdvd.h
raw_diff: fn_8018A514 implemented with BracketEntry/BracketSrcEntry loops and PAD_STACK(24)
raw_diff: fn_8018AA74 implemented with bracket slot positioning and HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y
raw_diff: fn_8018C8D4 and fn_8018D50C implemented DrawRectangle bracket-line drawing logic
text_corpus: bot report lists gm_18A5 new matches and improvements

key_files.role: Static header/type surface for gm_18A5; changed alongside the main implementation.
Public header updated alongside recovered gm_18A5 bodies.
Shared game-mode type definitions adjusted for the gm_18A5 work.
Small related game-mode tournament change; bot report says fn_8019CDBC reached 100%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists main/melee/gm/gmtou fn_8019CDBC +39 bytes, 92.21% to 100.00%

what_changed: Implemented many gm_18A5 functions that were previously placeholders, including examples visible in the diff such as fn_8018A514, fn_8018AA74, fn_8018C8D4, and fn_8018D50C.
Added required includes in gm_18A5.c for gobjplink.h and lbdvd.h.
Added or exposed several lbl_804DA6B4 through lbl_804DA6C0 extern string/data declarations in gm_18A5.c.
Added static GXColor literals lbl_804DA67C and lbl_804DA684 used by bracket drawing functions.
Updated gm_18A5.h, gm_18A5.static.h, and gm/types.h in parallel with the implementation work.
Made a small related gmtou.c change; the bot report identifies fn_8019CDBC as newly 100% matched.
Bot report recorded 18 new full matches and 46 improvements in unmatched items, with the largest listed scope in main/melee/gm/gm_18A5.

smart_moves.text: The PR updated headers and shared types alongside the recovered gm_18A5 bodies rather than keeping all declarations local to the C file.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: gm_18A5.h, gm_18A5.static.h, types.h all changed

smart_moves.text: The visible implementations use structured switch/case logic for bracket types and slot cases rather than leaving opaque branch-ladder placeholders.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8018AA74 switch(entry->x4) and nested switch(slot_idx)

smart_moves.text: The code maps JObj movement to existing HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y helpers in fn_8018AA74.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For large game-mode decomp PRs, update the relevant header and type surfaces when recovered bodies prove signatures or struct access needs; this PR changed gm_18A5.c together with gm_18A5.h, gm_18A5.static.h, and types.h.
Bracket-layout code in gm_18A5 appears reviewable when organized around typed-ish BracketEntry/BracketSrcEntry data, natural loops over entries, and switch cases for bracket/slot variants.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8018A514 uses BracketEntry, BracketSrcEntry, for loops over count/n
raw_diff: fn_8018AA74 uses entry_idx, slot_idx, and switch cases
raw_diff: fn_8018C8D4 comment says Draws tournament bracket lines for different bracket types (0-3)

decomp_lessons.lesson: A bot progress report is useful PR evidence for which functions reached 100% and which only improved, but the slice does not include local build/objdiff logs beyond that report.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report lists 18 new matches and 46 improvements
evidence_quality.notes: no human review comments and truncated diff

naming_conventions.name: entry_idx, slot_idx
naming_conventions.text: Visible gm_18A5 code uses short role names for bracket entry and slot indexing in fn_8018AA74.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: void fn_8018AA74(HSD_JObj* jobj, s32 entry_idx, s32 slot_idx)

naming_conventions.name: BracketEntry, BracketSrcEntry, TmData
naming_conventions.text: The implementation uses bracket and tournament data type names rather than only raw pointer variables, although many fields remain offset-style such as x0, x1C, x4C, and xA4.

naming_conventions.name: half_h, third_h, mid_y, bot_y, two_third_y, neg_thickness, total_width
naming_conventions.text: The bracket drawing code names geometry locals by their local drawing role.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8018C8D4 local declarations include half_h, third_h, mid_y, bot_y, two_third_y, neg_thickness, total_width

naming_conventions.name: fn_8018xxxx and lbl_80xxxxxx
naming_conventions.text: Unproven global/function symbols remain address-style, consistent with conservative naming when semantics are not fully proven.

evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists fn_8018A514 improved from 0.00% to 90.72%, not 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The loaded evidence does not include an objdiff explanation for PAD_STACK, and the bot report shows fn_8018A514 as improved but not fully matched. Treat as tactic evidence, not an accepted style rule.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Local pointer aliases and repeated stores are used to influence bracket-slot positioning codegen in fn_8018AA74.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: p34, p3C, p44, p38, p40, p48 derived from sub offsets
text_corpus: bot report lists fn_8018AA74 improved from 0.00% to 91.30%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This is supported as an observed tactic in this PR, but not as a recommended general style without verifier evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Static GXColor objects are introduced for repeated yellow draw colors in bracket drawing functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The slice shows the data additions but does not include section ownership or symbol metadata; do not generalize into a literal/data ownership rule.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: JObj translation operations are expressed through HSD_JObjSetTranslateX and HSD_JObjSetTranslateY.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This aligns with the loaded header-inlines standard when such helpers represent the source operation.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2239 gm_18A5 made broad game-mode matching progress with header/type updates
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2239 replaced many gm_18A5 placeholders with C implementations and updated gm_18A5.h, gm_18A5.static.h, types.h, and gmtou.c. The bot report recorded +7748 matched bytes, 18 new full matches, and 46 improvements.

accepted_candidate_records.title: gm_18A5 bracket code uses role locals plus address-style globals/functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: Visible bracket drawing and positioning functions use role locals such as entry_idx, slot_idx, half_h, third_h, mid_y, and neg_thickness while retaining fn_/lbl_ address-style symbols where broader semantics are not proven.

source_update_candidates.title: gm_18A5.c covers tournament bracket game-mode logic
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2239 evidence suggests src/melee/gm/gm_18A5.c includes tournament bracket layout/drawing logic, with BracketEntry/BracketSrcEntry data, DrawRectangle bracket-line functions, and comments describing tournament bracket lines/connectors.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: BracketEntry and BracketSrcEntry in fn_8018A514
raw_diff: comment 'Draws tournament bracket lines for different bracket types (0-3)'
raw_diff: comment 'Draws tournament bracket connector lines with optional tail segments'
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-level context, but should be curated against current source before becoming a path fact.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not treat PAD_STACK in improved-but-unmatched functions as self-justifying
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2239, fn_8018A514 visibly used PAD_STACK(24), while the bot report listed it as improved from 0.00% to 90.72%, not fully matched. This supports caution that PAD_STACK should remain tied to explicit verifier evidence and not be generalized from score improvement alone.
source_update_candidates.reason: This likely reinforces an existing standard rather than adding a new one; curator should decide whether to add as an example.

source_update_candidates.title: Check BracketEntry/BracketSrcEntry field meanings after gm_18A5 PR 2239
source_update_candidates.text: The PR uses many bracket entry fields and offsets, including x0-x6, xC/x10/x14/x18/x1C, color bytes, x4C/x78/xA4, and slot-stride offset math. Some may warrant data-sheet notes if current source and symbols confirm their meanings.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8018A514 copies BracketSrcEntry fields into BracketEntry fields
raw_diff: fn_8018AA74 computes slot offset as slot_idx * 0x2C
raw_diff: fn_8018C8D4 branches on x4C, x78, xA4
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR evidence exposes structure usage but does not prove semantic names for fields.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the static GXColor labels lbl_804DA67C/lbl_804DA684 as a general data ownership pattern; the slice lacks section ownership evidence.
Do not infer that every improved function in the bot report is matched; many are listed as partial improvements below 100%.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments are loaded.
Do not rename fn_/lbl_ address-style symbols from this PR alone; the visible code retains many address-style names and semantics are not fully proven.
